findings.title: JSON extraction duplication remains open
evidence: Current modules already delegate to a shared utility in utils/json.py, so the report's claim of four independent full implementations is stale.
The shared utility is still object-centered and lacks parse_json_payload and a typed extraction error requested by the implementation plan.
Array payload and multi-brace prose behavior are not covered by the requested new test surface.
findings.planned_action: Extend the shared JSON extraction utility surface without breaking existing wrapper functions. Add tests for objects, arrays, fenced blocks, embedded JSON, malformed JSON, empty text, and multiple brace-like sections.
findings.risk_if_unfixed: LLM response parsing remains underspecified for arrays and malformed payload diagnostics, even though the worst duplication risk has already been reduced.
tests_required: existing JSON utility and KG extraction tests
findings.documentation_report_impact: Triage and remediation summary only; no scientific metric changes.

findings.title: _window_text soft-break boundary behavior
evidence: _window_text already has a word-boundary fallback, contradicting the report's claim that it is unchanged.

The current implementation still combines paragraph, sentence, and word breaks under one max_chars//2 threshold instead of explicitly preferring paragraph, then sentence, then whitespace with a separate min_end policy.
findings.planned_action: Refactor boundary selection into explicit helpers that preserve progress and char ranges while preferring paragraph, sentence, then whitespace boundaries.
findings.risk_if_unfixed: Chunk boundaries can remain less readable than necessary for fixed-small style chunking, affecting retrieval diagnostics but not existing reported metrics unless reports are regenerated.
findings.documentation_report_impact: No metric change unless chunking reports are explicitly regenerated.

findings.title: NDCG / gold matching may false-match source_page=-1
evidence: _hit_matches_gold returns False when gold.source_page < 0 before page fallback matching.
No-answer labels are excluded from normal retrieval targets before page matching.
Existing edge-case tests cover page-unset behavior.
findings.planned_action: No code change unless tests reveal a regression during Phase 1 verification.
tests_required: rerun full metric tests after related changes
findings.documentation_report_impact: Record as already fixed in remediation summary.

findings.title: LLM evidence strict substring containment remains brittle
evidence: Strict exact containment remains the acceptance criterion, which is intentional for provenance safety.
Current code already records filtered triple counts, filter reasons, and diagnostic evidence near-misses using token-overlap metadata.
The requested normalized/fuzzy evidence helpers are not yet exposed as a clear evidence matching policy surface.
findings.planned_action: Keep exact provenance as the only accepted KG evidence, but add named exact/normalized/fuzzy diagnostic helpers and tests in Phase 2 if Phase 1 remains stable.
findings.risk_if_unfixed: Diagnostics are less explicit than they could be, but accepting fuzzy evidence without a policy would risk provenance overclaiming.
tests_required: fuzzy match above and below threshold
findings.documentation_report_impact: Remediation must state fuzzy/normalized matches are diagnostic only.

findings.title: _proposition_segments may emit short first segment
evidence: _proposition_segments already forward-merges leading short fragments while more than one segment exists.
tests/test_chunking.py contains test_proposition_like_merges_leading_short_fragment.
findings.planned_action: No code change beyond rerunning chunking tests after C2 changes.
tests_required: add middle/last/only-short tests only if new failures are found
findings.documentation_report_impact: Record as already fixed in remediation summary.

findings.title: _source_set uses '+' delimiter ambiguously
evidence: _source_set currently splits source strings on a literal '+'.
Current source labels do not contain '+', but the invariant is not documented or guarded.
findings.planned_action: Introduce a delimiter constant and source merge helper with tests for duplicates, empty source, and invalid labels while preserving the public source string format.
findings.risk_if_unfixed: Future source labels containing '+' could be split incorrectly and misreported.
tests_required: invalid source label with delimiter raises or is sanitized
findings.documentation_report_impact: No metric impact; remediation summary records invariant.

findings.title: llm_runtime_available duplicates provider lookup
evidence: llm_runtime_available still reads os.getenv('LLM_PROVIDER', 'openai').lower() directly.
llm/providers.py exposes configured_llm_provider for the same policy.
findings.planned_action: Use configured_llm_provider in llm_runtime_available and preserve existing API-key checks.
findings.risk_if_unfixed: Provider handling could drift if central provider logic changes.
tests_required: missing API key behavior remains unchanged

findings.title: generate_ontology raises generic RuntimeError after partial output
evidence: Partial TTL and manifests are already written on failure, so the original data-loss risk is addressed.
Failure paths still raise RuntimeError instead of a typed exception carrying partial paths for programmatic callers.
findings.planned_action: Defer until after P1/P2 unless time permits; if implemented, add OntologyGenerationError without changing CLI behavior.
findings.risk_if_unfixed: Programmatic callers must read partial output paths after catching RuntimeError.

findings.title: _score silently returns 0.0 for missing or invalid scores
evidence: _score returns 0.0 for missing, None, or non-numeric score values.
This behavior is deterministic and probably acceptable, but not documented or directly tested.
findings.planned_action: Document _score behavior and add direct tests for missing score, non-numeric score, numeric strings, and rank-only items.
findings.risk_if_unfixed: Future changes could misinterpret unscored duplicate-hit merge behavior.

findings.title: load_questions_and_gold_labels may load gold labels twice
evidence: When gold labels exist but lack embedded question text, load_questions_and_gold_labels loads labels once for inspection and again through gold_labels_for_questions.
Current benchmark size makes this a minor latency issue, but avoiding double parse is straightforward.
findings.planned_action: Allow gold_labels_for_questions to accept already-loaded labels and add a spy-loader test proving one load in both branches.
findings.risk_if_unfixed: Avoidable duplicate parsing on fallback path.
tests_required: labels with question text load once
labels without question text load once and overlay boundary questions
missing labels still raise expected error

phase_plan: Phase 1: JSON extraction surface, chunk boundary behavior, and verification that I3/M3 remain fixed.
Phase 2: Evidence matching diagnostics and retrieval source/score robustness.
Phase 3: Provider lookup consistency, gold label loader double-load, optional ontology typed exception.
Phase 4: Remediation summary plus dashboard/final-report wording only if implementation-review remediation should be surfaced.
